rulings.Case Title: KEVIN STUCKART et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MAY 22, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 7, Defendant CNH America, LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR GOMEZ VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MAY 22, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Exxon Mobil Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the issue of asbestos content of the insulation, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from sustaining its burden. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, plaintiff¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. As to duty, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that it owed no duty to plaintiff. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MELVIN DESIN VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court¿S Order Granting Plaintiff¿S Motion For Preferential Trial Setting
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MAY 22, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s amended motion for reconsideration of the court¿s order granting plaintiff¿s motion for preferential trial setting is granted. Defendant demonstrated new facts as required by C.C.P. 1008 (a) ¿ Dr. Yom¿s deposition, which occurred subsequent to this court¿s ruling on plaintiff¿s preference motion. Upon reconsideration, the court affirms its order granting preference pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a) & (d). Considering both Dr. Yom¿s declaration and deposition testimony, plaintiff met the requirements of C.C.P. 36(a) & (d). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES WOODYARD VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MAY 22, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 6, Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.¿s general demurrer to plaintiff¿s complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiff an opportunity, if he is able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court.
